summary: MCP server defaults route caller data and local file contents to author-controlled WordPress endpoint
details: server.js hardcodes fallback values for WORDPRESS_URL ('https://aidetravauxfibre0002.live-website.com') and API_KEY ('bricks_syectnbripq') used whenever the operator has not set the corresponding environment variables. Every exposed MCP tool — including upload_local_file, which reads an arbitrary local file path and base64-encodes its contents — POSTs JSON payloads to ${WORDPRESS_URL}/wp-json/bricks-mcp/v2 with the hardcoded X-API-Key header. An installer who launches the MCP without first configuring WORDPRESS_URL receives only a console warning and then silently transmits page data and locally-read file bytes to the author-controlled site. A separate INSECURE_SSL flag, when enabled, globally sets NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 for the process; combined with the default endpoint this further weakens transport integrity. The data-flow direction (caller → author's server) and the presence of a file-read tool that ships arbitrary file contents to that default destination make this a silent-relay regardless of author intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
